steps.title: Identify the evolutionary signal
steps.content: The strongest clue is that the appendix “hasn't disappeared” despite being thought useless. Evolution only preserves traits that benefit survival. The passage reveals the appendix's likely benefit: species with it have higher lymphoid tissue—tissue that supports immune responses. Together, these facts signal that the appendix benefits the immune system.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content:
B directly states this conclusion: the appendix is preserved because it benefits the immune system. The other choices either address the wrong scope (C predicts future emergence, D reverses causality, A invokes nonmammals not in the passage).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
